currentSliceTitle: Suppress visible explored terrain grid seams
currentSliceNotes: Completed a narrow presentation-only correction for AcOrP feedback that black lines remained between visible explored overworld tiles. OverworldMapView no longer draws explicit per-tile black grid rectangles over explored terrain; explored terrain now reports and uses a fog-boundary-only seam model while unexplored hidden terrain keeps its wireframe treatment. Smoke metadata/assertions now guard the lack of inter-explored tile seams and the preserved unexplored wireframe. Gameplay, pathing, fog, save, object, town, hero, site logic, terrain palettes, and road rules were unchanged. Required validation passed: python3 tests/validate_repo.py; godot4 --headless --path . res://tests/overworld_visual_smoke.tscn; godot4 --headless --path . res://tests/ninefold_scenario_smoke.tscn; git diff --check.

realityAssessment.notes: Existing systems and validation harnesses remain useful foundations, but do not count as product completion until proven through live-client manual play.

phases.title: Honest reset, parity ledger, and removal of fake-complete language
phases.focus: Planning docs now state the prototype/pre-alpha reality and reset the roadmap around manual play evidence.

phases.title: Make River Pass manually completable end-to-end
phases.focus: AcOrP reported River Pass manual completion gate passed on 2026-04-18, covering the live-client overworld, town, battle, save/resume, victory, defeat, and outcome-routing gate.

phases.title: Playable alpha baseline with 2 fully realized factions
phases.focus: Use the faction bible as the six-faction content scaffold source, then deepen selected alpha factions through scenario, town, battle, AI, save/load, and manual-play slices before claiming playability.

phases.title: Production alpha layer for external playtest readiness
phases.focus: Coherent placeholder art/audio, settings, export/build pipeline, logs, onboarding, and external playtest workflow.

phases.title: HoMM2-class breadth
phases.focus: Playable broad content package with multiple factions, maps, campaigns, AI, and reliable save/load.

phases.title: HoMM3-class breadth
phases.focus: Later strategic depth expansion after HoMM2-class breadth is stable and playable.

activeMilestone.goal: A real player can complete River Pass from the shipped live client without editor-only steps or developer-only knowledge.
acceptanceCriteria: River Pass launches from the live menu path chosen for the milestone.
The opening UI communicates objective, threat, and a useful first action.
The overworld map is readable enough for movement, route choice, site interaction, town entry, and encounter entry.
At least one owned town supports useful recruitment or recovery decisions that affect completion.
At least one battle is entered through normal overworld play and can be resolved through visible tactical controls.
Battle state clearly exposes active side, legal actions, target selection, and result.
Post-battle survivors and losses sync back to overworld state.
One victory path is reachable by normal play.
One defeat path is reachable and routes coherently.
Save/resume works from overworld, town, and battle states used by the scenario.
Victory and defeat outcomes route through the normal outcome and menu flow.
The scenario is manually completed at least twice from a clean profile with notes captured.

steps.title: Reset project and plan docs to the prototype/pre-alpha reality
Draft a six-faction content bible with no commons or shared faction skeletons
Start real six-faction implementation with JSON content scaffolds and validation
Define overworld biomes, objects, buildings, and resource grammar
Implement biome and richer overworld object content domains
Implement first real neutral dwellings and neutral units for overworld and battle
Expand neutral dwellings to 25 authored content families
Author one 64x64 scenario that places the broad faction, biome, dwelling, and site-family scaffold
Fix overworld default framing so large maps open at a tactical zoom instead of fitting the whole map
Fix remembered-object readability, large-map panning, and remote owned-town entry
Improve overworld object marker readability on visible and remembered tiles
Keep explored overworld terrain fully visible outside the current scout net
Allow diagonal overworld movement at the same one-point cost as cardinal movement
Prepare and wire newly cut overworld terrain and object assets
Replace sampled terrain textures with an authored terrain grammar foundation
Use first authored overworld terrain tile art in the renderer
Replace terrain tile art with generated source terrain cuts
Correct overworld terrain away from generated-source tiling
Ground overworld markers into terrain instead of UI plates
Push overworld markers toward object-first placed presence
Deepen overworld terrain-contact and depth cues
Add footprint-aware overworld placement beds
Add upper-mass overworld backdrop cues
Integrate first town and encounter overworld placeholder assets
Present towns as 3x2 overworld footprints with a bottom-middle approach tile
Quiet town overworld grounding without base ellipses
Present the active hero as a placed overworld presence instead of a staged badge
Correct base terrain and structural road tile readability
Correct grass/plains consistency, terrain transitions, and road intersections after AcOrP feedback
Suppress black seams between visible explored overworld terrain tiles
Run a live-client manual audit of River Pass and record the first real player blockers
Create the River Pass parity ledger for overworld, town, battle, save/resume, victory, and defeat
Fix launch, objective, opening guidance, and overworld readability blockers
Recover owned-town usability for recruitment, recovery, garrison, and return routing
Recover battle usability for turn clarity, legal actions, targeting, enemy turns, and battle resolution
Tune River Pass so one fair victory path and one coherent defeat path are reachable
Prove save/resume from overworld, town, and battle during the real scenario path
Prove victory and defeat outcome routing plus restart and menu return behavior
Complete River Pass manually at least twice from a clean profile and capture remaining friction

nextActions: Manually inspect the visible explored terrain seam correction in River Pass and Ninefold Confluence at live-client scale before treating the black-grid feedback as fully solved across supported viewports.

Manually inspect the corrected cohesive grasslands base, softened edge overlays, and connection-aware road joints in River Pass and Ninefold Confluence at live-client scale before treating the terrain/road immersion issue as fully solved.
Manually inspect the corrected active-hero placed-figure presentation in River Pass and Ninefold Confluence before treating hero presence as polished across every biome and supported resolution.
Plan a future explicit multi-tile town occupancy/pathing slice before making non-entry town footprint cells affect movement, blocking, save schema, or visit resolution.

Manually inspect the corrected no-base-ellipse town presentation plus the hostile_camp overworld placeholder in River Pass and Ninefold Confluence before calling the object-art direction visually polished.

Add future authored faction-specific town variants, encounter-family variants, and high-priority site sprites so the object-first renderer is not carrying important player-facing breadth through generic placeholders or procedural silhouettes.
Add future authored town/object footprint or sprite slices so unmapped procedural silhouettes can be replaced where player-facing objects need richer presence.
Add future authored tile-art slices for water/coast, badlands/wastes, ash/lava, snow/frost, and cavern/underway before treating Ninefold Confluence's full terrain surface as art-covered.
Run a manual or routed live-client audit of Ninefold Confluence before treating the 64x64 breadth map as playable, tuned, or route-proven.
Manually inspect Ninefold Confluence's panning and remembered-object readability in the live client at the supported 16:9 runtime resolutions before treating the large-map UX as broadly polished.
Deepen selected neutral dwelling families in Ninefold Confluence with encounter tuning and manual play notes before claiming balance or player-facing readiness.
Define truthful paired transit behavior before making Ninefold Confluence ferry or rope-lift placements route-critical.
Implement paired transit traversal or guarded-bank encounter resolution before placing transit objects or guarded reward sites as player-facing route-critical content.
Integrate selected scaffold factions into scenario placements, army groups, encounters, overworld landmarks, AI fronts, and manual play notes.
Capture a short parity-ledger note for River Pass recording AcOrP's passed manual gate so the milestone stays documented beyond chat history.
Avoid pretending HoMM3-class overworld breadth exists until richer biomes, objects, and repeated manual-play scenarios actually land.
